audits.summary: No publicly accessible audit report found for Notcoin (NOT) Jetton contract on TON via GitHub, project docs, or major audit firm public registries as of mid-2025.
audits.url: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — no audit PDF/repo link discoverable via free sources

exploit_history.description: No exploits or hacks involving Notcoin (NOT) found on Rekt.news or DefiLlama Hacks page as of research date (2025-07). The project has had no publicly documented smart-contract exploit. Note: centralized distribution risks existed during the airdrop phase (May 2024) but no funds were lost to a contract exploit.

code_health.primary_repo_url: https://github.com/notcoin-bot — NOTE: primary Jetton contract source not prominently open-sourced on GitHub; TON contract address: EQAvlWFDxGF2lXm67y4yzC17wYKD9A0guwPkMs1gOsM6vBGS (mainnet)
code_health.contributors_count: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — GitHub org has limited public repos; contributor count for core token contract not determinable
code_health.weekly_commits_avg: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — no active public development repo with commit history found for the token contract
code_health.bug_bounty_platform: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — no Immunefi or HackenProof public listing found for Notcoin as of 2025-07

dependencies.notes: NOT token is a TON Jetton; security is entirely dependent on TON L1 consensus and the TON Jetton standard. TON has a smaller security researcher ecosystem than EVM, reducing likelihood of independent vulnerability discovery. TON has not suffered a major L1 exploit to date.

dependencies.provider: TON Bridge (ton.org/bridge) for EVM-side wrapped NOT
dependencies.notes: Bridged NOT on EVM chains (e.g., via official TON Bridge or third-party bridges) introduces bridge smart-contract risk. TON Bridge is operated by the TON Foundation; no public audit link found via free sources. Bridge exploits are a common attack vector industry-wide.

Notcoin (NOT) is a pure transfer/meme token with no on-chain DeFi logic requiring price oracles in its native contract. Oracle risk exists only in third-party DeFi protocols that list NOT as collateral (e.g., DEXes on TON like STON.fi or DeDust), not in the token contract itself.

rationale: Notcoin (NOT) presents a relatively low smart-contract attack surface because it is a simple TON Jetton (fungible token) with no complex DeFi logic, upgradability, or admin keys embedded in the token contract itself — reducing rug-pull and exploit vectors at the contract level. However, the security posture is difficult to verify independently because no public audit report, bug bounty program, or open-source contract repository with commit history was discoverable via free sources, which is a significant transparency gap for a token with multi-billion dollar market cap at peak. The primary residual risks are TON ecosystem immaturity (smaller auditor pool), bridge risk for EVM-side liquidity, and the centralized off-chain game infrastructure that controlled the original point-to-token conversion.
sources: https://github.com/notcoin-bot — limited public repos, no audit report found
https://notco.in — official project site (no audit links found in docs)
